description: Attributes for response of creating a new incident (POST-method).

id.x-custom-comment: Restricted to federated SMC specific pattern

severity.x-custom-comment: Restricted to federated SMC specific pattern
severity.description:
TM Forum severity corresponds to ITIL incident impact.
- high - TM Forum severity corresponds to ITIL incident impact. Definition for high: Another MNE is affected and the overall impact is high.
- medium - Definition for medium: Another MNE is affected and the overall impact is medium or low.
- low - Definition for high: Another MNE is NOT affected and the local impact is high, medium or low.

creationDate.x-custom-comment: changed from 'date' to 'date-time' due to service interface profile

status.x-custom-comment: Restricted to federated SMC specific status values. InProgressHeld and InProgressPending are moved to new property 'subStatus'
status.description:
The current status of the incident. If the status is "InProgress" the
subStatus field might be used to indicated a pending situation.
- Submitted - The initial state of an Incident when created by a TT
originator.
- Rejected - The Trouble Ticket was rejected because it:
  - is not submitted
  - provides invalid information
  - fails to meet the Business Rules in respect of the Product which originator is raising a Trouble Ticket against
  - is otherwise defective
- Acknowledged - The Incident was accepted and allocated a unique
Incident ID by the Incident handler.
- InProgress - The Incident was validated by the Incident handler and is
being processed.
- Resolved - The Fault indicated in the Incident was corrected by the
Incident handler and acknowledgement is awaited from its originator.
- Closed - The Incident originator has acknowledged the Resolved
state of the Incident, or the timeframe (default value 14 days) for
acknowledgement has passed without response from TT originator.
- Cancelled - The Incident which was sent from the originator to the
Incident handler was technically formatted correctly and was wherefore
acknowledged in first place, but the content on the Incident is
inadequate. Therefore, the Incident handler rejects to work on this
Incident.  Reasons for Incident cancellation are:
  - wrongly assigned
  - information provided is inadequate.
